sourceMovie.title: Gravity
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Gravity works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Martian
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Martian (2015) belongs to the same conversation as Gravity — both anchor their science-fiction in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Iron Giant
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gravity's science-fiction stayed with you, The Iron Giant (1999) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The drama through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Pitch Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Gravity excels in science-fiction, Pitch Black (2000) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan (1982) is the natural next step after Gravity: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Gravity who respond to its science-fiction will find Star Trek III: The Search for Spock (1984) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Star Trek: Generations
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Gravity fans keep landing on Star Trek: Generations (1994) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Limey
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Limey draws from the same thriller well as Gravity, channeling drama in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1999 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Life
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Life (2017) captures the science-fiction appeal of Gravity without simply imitating it. The horror streak running through both films is what puts Life at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Wrestler
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Gravity — that bittersweet edge, the romance — translates cleanly into The Wrestler (2008). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Event Horizon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Event Horizon (1997) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Gravity rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

faq.question: What should I watch after Gravity?
faq.answer: Try The Martian, The Iron Giant, Pitch Black—each shares DNA with Gravity while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Gravity?
faq.answer: Gravity (2013) is best described as Science Fiction, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Gravity worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
